--- title: "Exploring Names" subtitle: "Unit 1 Task 1" author: "Sydney Ahlstrom Sanders" format: html: self-contained: true page-layout: full title-block-banner: true toc: true toc-depth: 3 toc-location: body number-sections: false html-math-method: katex code-fold: true code-summary: "Show the code" code-overflow: wrap code-copy: hover code-tools: source: false toggle: true caption: See code execute: kernel: python311 warning: false --- ::: {#ac91dc78 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import polars as pl import numpy as np from lets_plot import * LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True) ``` ::: ::: {#c116bc05 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Learn morea about Code Cells: https://quarto.org/docs/reference/cells/cells-jupyter.html # Include and execute your code here df = pl.read_csv("https://posit.byui.edu/content/0d43d66f-d3b7-4c43-8c36-52e3ed69e7f1/names_year_csv.csv") ``` ::: ## QUESTION 1 __What was the earliest year that the name ‘Felisha’ was used?__ _The earliest year the name Felisha was used was in 1964._ ::: {#3f31646d .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Q1 felisha_year = ( df.filter(pl.col("name") == "Felisha") .select(pl.col("year").min()) ) felisha_year ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
shape: (1, 1)
year
i64
1964
``` ::: ::: ## QUESTION 2 __What year had the most babies named 'David'? How many babies were named 'David' that year?__ _In 1955 there were the most babies named David. 86389 babies were named David that year._ ::: {#ec89e39f .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Q2 print(type(df)) print(pl.__version__) david_peak = ( df.filter(pl.col("name") == "David") .group_by("year") .agg(pl.col("Total").sum()) .sort("Total", descending=True) .head(1) ) david_peak ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` 1.37.1 ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
shape: (1, 2)
yearTotal
i64i64
195586389
``` ::: ::: ## QUESTION 3 __What year did your name hit its peak? How many babies were named your name in that year?__ _The use of my name, Sydney, peaked in the year 2000. 10297 babies were named Sydney that year, and I was one of the babies named that the next year!_ ::: {#9b5bd6f8 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Q3 my_name = "Sydney" sydney_peak = ( df.filter(pl.col("name") == my_name) .group_by("year") .agg(pl.col("Total").sum()) .sort("Total", descending=True) .head(1) ) sydney_peak ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ```{=html}
shape: (1, 2)
yearTotal
i64i64
200010297
``` ::: ::: ## QUESTION 4 __In the most recent year, what was the most common female name in Utah?__ _I had a hard time figuring out how to sort names between male and female, so instead I just did the top 10 names in Utah in the most recent year. My best guess for which name is "female" is Olivia, but technically Oliver was the top name used._ ::: {#2aafff2f .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} #Q4 latest_year = df.select(pl.col("year").max()).item() utah_latest = ( df.filter(pl.col("year") == latest_year) .sort("UT", descending=True) .head(10) .select(["name", "UT", "year"]) ) utah_latest ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ```{=html}
